title: Quick Emulsion PCR Extraction Protocol
author: Vijay K Chaudhary
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcjpiumn
materials: Vigourously vortex the tube for 1 min.

steps.title: Transfer entire emulsion PCR mixture (100...
steps.instruction: Transfer entire emulsion PCR mixture (100 µl + the overlaid mineral oil) to a fresh 1.5 ml microfuge tube and add 500 µl of QIA PB purification buffer (5 x volume) directly the tube. For larger ePCR mixture, appropriate volume of PB buffer can be added.

steps.title: Load the entire suspension on the QIAquick column
steps.instruction: Load the entire suspension on the QIAquick column. Centrifuge at 13,000g for 1 min at room temperature (25°C). Discard the flow-through. This step may be repeated if the suspension is more than 700 µl.

steps.title: Centrifuge the suspension at 13,000g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the suspension at 13,000g for 1 min at room temperature (25°C).

steps.title: Add 700 µl of QIA PE buffer to the column
steps.instruction: Add 700 µl of QIA PE buffer to the column. Centrifuge at 13,000g for 1 min at room temperature (25°C). Discard the flow-through.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13,000g for 2 min...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13,000g for 2 min at room temperature (25°C) to remove excess ethanol. Transfer the column to a fresh microfuge tube.

steps.title: Add 40 µl of QIA EB...
steps.instruction: Add 40 µl of QIA EB buffer to the column and incubate at room temperature (25°C) for 5 mins. Centrifuge at 13,000g for 1 min at room temperature (25°C).

steps.title: The eluted DNA is ready to...
steps.instruction: The eluted DNA is ready to use for downstream work and can be quantified using agarose gel electrophoresis or Nanodrop or any other method available with the user.
